components.title: Nutanix AHV
components.description: Nutanix AHV, the built-in KVM-based hypervisor of the Nutanix hyperconverged infrastructure (HCI) platform, providing compute virtualisation tightly integrated with the Nutanix Distributed Storage Fabric (DSF) across a cluster of Nutanix nodes. Managed centrally via Prism Central (multi-cluster management) and Prism Element (per-cluster management), with cluster-wide data-at-rest encryption and role-based access control integrated with the organisation's identity provider.
components.purpose: Provide hyperconverged compute and storage virtualisation for organisational server workloads, with built-in resilience, data services (snapshots, replication, deduplication), and simplified lifecycle management via the Nutanix platform.

props.name: deployment-model
props.value: Hyperconverged infrastructure — AHV hypervisor integrated with the Nutanix Distributed Storage Fabric across clustered nodes

props.name: management
props.value: Prism Central (multi-cluster) and Prism Element (per-cluster) management interfaces

props.name: security-features
props.value: Cluster-wide data-at-rest encryption (software or SED-based), Security Configuration Management Automation (SCMA), micro-segmentation via Flow Network Security

props.name: identity-platform
props.value: Role-based access control integrated with Active Directory / LDAP / SAML SSO

protocols.name: https
protocols.title: HTTPS — Prism Central / Prism Element management interface

protocols.name: ssh
protocols.title: SSH — CLI administration of Controller VMs (CVMs) and AHV hosts

protocols.name: https
protocols.title: HTTPS — Nutanix REST API for automation and orchestration integration

protocols.name: ntp
protocols.title: NTP — Time synchronisation across cluster nodes and CVMs

protocols.name: syslog
protocols.title: Syslog — Cluster and security event forwarding to SIEM

implemented-requirements.description: An approved hardening baseline for the Nutanix AHV cluster is developed and maintained, based on Nutanix Security Configuration Management Automation (SCMA) guidance benchmarked against DISA STIG/CIS-equivalent controls, applied consistently across AHV hosts and Controller VMs (CVMs).

Nutanix AHV hosts and Controller VMs are hardened per Nutanix SCMA guidance: unused services are disabled, SSH access to CVMs is restricted to designated administrator accounts, and cluster communication is isolated to a dedicated management/storage VLAN.

Cluster-wide data-at-rest encryption is enabled on the Nutanix Distributed Storage Fabric (software-based encryption or self-encrypting drives), with encryption keys managed via the Nutanix native key manager or an external KMIP-compliant key management server.

Nutanix cluster administrators are assigned dedicated privileged accounts, separate from their standard user accounts, used solely for Prism Central/Prism Element and cluster administration, managed via Nutanix role-based access control.
Multi-factor authentication is enforced for administrators authenticating to Prism Central and Prism Element, via SAML SSO integration with the organisation's MFA-enforcing identity provider.

Security-relevant Nutanix cluster events (authentication attempts, VM lifecycle operations, cluster configuration and Flow Network Security policy changes) are centrally logged via Prism and forwarded to the SIEM.

Virtual machine data is protected via Nutanix native snapshots and asynchronous or near-synchronous replication to a secondary Nutanix cluster or cloud target, retained per business continuity requirements.

A change and configuration management plan governs the Nutanix cluster baseline, including the approved SCMA hardening profile, AOS/AHV upgrade (LCM) schedule, and Flow Network Security micro-segmentation policy, reviewed at least annually.
